CLARITY.justification: The paper is well-organized (Intro → Related → Method → Experiments → Discussion) and explicitly lists its main contributions early (bullet points in the Introduction). Notation and equations are generally consistent and defined (e.g., ancestral/fraternal states hai,hfi, predictive state h(pred)i, topology probabilities pai,pfi), and detailed training/model specifications in the Appendix improve clarity. Figures/tables and concrete examples are referred to throughout and the experimental descriptions are thorough, though a few long/complex sentences, occasional minor grammatical/formatting hiccups, and some reliance on figures not present in the provided text slightly detract from readability.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper proposes a distinct architecture (Doubly Recurrent Neural Networks) that explicitly maintains separate ancestral and fraternal recurrences and combines them per-node (Eqs. 1–3) and an explicit topological prediction mechanism (Sec. 3.1, Eqs. 5–7). The authors clearly differentiate their approach from very recent related work (e.g. Zhang et al. 2016 and Dong & Lapata 2016) by emphasizing simultaneous depth/width recurrences, explicit topology prediction (instead of special padding tokens), and end-to-end training. These are non-trivial methodological extensions rather than mere engineering tweaks, and represent a notable, though not earth‑shattering, advance over prior tree-decoding efforts.

SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The core technical claims are clearly stated and supported by coherent mathematical definitions and a plausible training scheme (DRNN cell equations (1)-(7) and BPTS in Sec. 3.2 / Appendix B). The experimental methodology is generally sound: synthetic recovery (Sec. 4.1) with a probabilistic baseline and real-data comparisons on IFTTT (Sec. 4.2) against prior work (SEQ2SEQ/SEQ2TREE), and qualitative MT analyses (Sec. 4.3). Minor issues remain — no statistical-significance tests or ablation studies are reported, the PCFG baseline description/advantage is somewhat ambiguous, and reliance on teacher-forcing/topology golds (acknowledged) leaves exposure-bias effects unexplored — but these do not undermine the main technical conclusions.
